blender_collections.note: low/high/alpha are child collections of the 'Baking' root. The actual collection names are 'low'/'high'/'alpha', not 'Baking/low'. Role classification is by COLLECTION MEMBERSHIP, not by object-name suffix.

on_disk_layout.base: the folder that contains the .blend file
on_disk_layout.asset: the .blend filename stem (cleaned) - NOT a per-object name

naming.back_texture_set_behavior: Texture Sets ending in _back are backsides. They follow Low Poly as High change behavior, so low changes rebake instead of reload-only. The matching front Texture Set is the same name without _back; for example sibuki_01_back uses sibuki_01 as the Normal mesh-map source.
naming.texture_set_from_material: strip exactly one leading 'M_' from the Blender low material name (name[2:] when it startswith 'M_')

role_pairing_suffix.behavior: low<->high (baking) and low<->alpha (alpha detail) objects are paired by stripping a trailing role token and comparing the remaining base. The separator is optional and may be '_', '.', '-', a space, or nothing; an optional trailing number is allowed, so rock_high, rock_high_01 and rock_high1 all reduce to base 'rock'. A Blender '.001' duplicate suffix is stripped first. Matching is case-insensitive.
role_pairing_suffix.example: rock_low pairs with rock_high and rock_alpha
role_pairing_suffix.note: This suffix exists for PAIRING across roles, not for deciding which collection an object belongs to (that is collection membership).

json_ipc.atomic_write: each file is written to a '.<name>.tmp' sibling then os.replace()d into place

pending_request.location: %LOCALAPPDATA%/SubstanceTools/ (falls back to %TEMP%, then the user home)

painter_request.note: written to BOTH the texture dir and the low dir

status_values_in_use.SUCCESS: operation completed; Blender treats any value other than 'SUCCESS' as failure
status_values_in_use.ERROR: operation ran but did not succeed
status_values_in_use.FAILED: request could not be processed; marks the request so it is NOT retried forever

json_ipc.status_and_message_live_in: the export RESULT file (.substance_tools_export_result.json), written by the Painter plugin and read by Blender (result.get('status'), result.get('message'))

staleness_marker.behavior: Painter compares request_id (falling back to pipeline_hash) against the last one it processed (_last_polled_pipeline_hash) and skips a request it already handled. request_id is str(time.time_ns()).
staleness_marker.note: Stale-vs-current detection is ALREADY implemented via these markers - it is not a gap.

proposed_not_yet_implemented.note: Genuine gaps only. NOT written by the code today - do not assume present.

unreal_handoff_sidecar.purpose: Bridge UE Unique validation/material data to Send to Unreal and Unreal material post-import setup. Users are not expected to edit these files manually.

matching.individual_mesh: When Send to Unreal imports meshes individually, the sidecar name matches the mesh object/imported asset name.
matching.empty_combined_mesh: When Send to Unreal combines child meshes under an Empty, the sidecar name matches the Empty/imported asset name.
matching.reason_for_multiple_files: The producer writes both mesh-name and Empty-name sidecars so the pipeline survives either Send to Unreal export mode.

top_level_fields.material_master: default material master preset for the sidecar, currently 'prop'
top_level_fields.mesh_name: mesh or Empty asset name this sidecar is intended to match
top_level_fields.asset_prefix: cleaned blend-file/prefix value used by the handoff
top_level_fields.materials: array of material slot pipeline entries
top_level_fields.cleanup: object with precomputed source asset names that Send2UE/Unreal may delete from the imported mesh folder after material setup
top_level_fields.validation: optional per-mesh validation row for individual mesh sidecars
top_level_fields.validation_children: optional array of validation rows for Empty/group sidecars
top_level_fields.transfer_sources: optional array of transfer postprocess requests for Empty/group sidecars

material_entry_fields.name: Blender material name, expected to start with M_ when processed by Unreal
material_entry_fields.slot_index: best-effort material slot index; Unreal also matches by material name where possible
material_entry_fields.translucent: boolean; translucent entries may use the shared glass material path in Unreal
material_entry_fields.textures: legacy flat texture array; may be empty
material_entry_fields.layers: surface-layer texture array; may be empty
material_entry_fields.master_preset: optional preset key such as prop, cloth, layer, asset_surface, or coat

texture_entry_fields.param: Unreal material parameter name such as Albedo, Extra, Normal, Transmission, Emissive
texture_entry_fields.asset_name: Texture asset name to import/create in Unreal
texture_entry_fields.file: absolute texture file path written with forward slashes
texture_entry_fields.source_param: optional original Blender role when param was remapped

cleanup_fields.source_material_names: deduplicated source material asset names that FBX import may create in the mesh folder and that can be removed after material instances are assigned
cleanup_fields.source_texture_names: deduplicated source texture asset names that FBX import may create in the mesh folder and that can be removed after textures are imported to the canonical texture folder

transfer_request_fields.source: Blender transfer source object name from Vertex Data Tools
transfer_request_fields.shape_keys: boolean, request Shape Key transfer before mesh export
transfer_request_fields.weights: boolean, request robust weight transfer before mesh export
transfer_request_fields.enabled: boolean, true only when a source exists and at least one transfer operation is requested

validation_policy.textureless_materials: Allowed. They produce warnings in UE Unique but do not block JSON; Unreal can still create/assign an MI with empty texture parameters.
blocking_errors: unclean target/material names that would change on write
missing texture file paths or missing texture files when textures are present

current_default.implemented_in: BlenderTools send2ue core/utilities.py sync_unreal_mesh_folder_path() (single hardcoded anchor)

unreal_path_mapping.risk: A single hardcoded anchor is fragile when a local subtree does not mirror the Unreal content path. This already broke the character/ subtree once.
unreal_path_mapping.target_direction: Support multiple mappings and/or a sidecar file next to the .blend, and fail loudly when no mapping matches instead of silently using a plausible-but-wrong path.

minimum_real_round_trip: Update Painter on an existing project
Confirm low reload without mesh-map bake for High-to-Low low-only edits
Confirm rebake for Low Poly as High low edits
Confirm _back Texture Sets use the matching front Normal mesh map while baking the remaining mesh maps
Send to Unreal handoff through the Export collection
